meta.title: The ability of cholestyramine resin and other adsorbents to bind Escherichia coli enterotoxins -- Mullan et al. 12 (4): 487 -- Journal of Medical Microbiology

abstractHtml: Several adsorbent materials were evaluated for their ability to bind Escherichia coli enterotoxins. Cholestyramine, a strong anion-exchange resin, bound the heat-labile and the heat-stable types of enterotoxin and reduced significantly their effects in some animal models. However, its efficacy in the treatment of diarrhoeic piglets appeared to be adversely affected by the presence of milk in the alimentary tract.
